company.name: Brigade Electronics
company.description: Global manufacturer of vehicle safety systems for commercial vehicles and machinery — cameras, sensors, radar, AI vision, and white-sound reversing alarms

primary.name: Deep Navy
primary.usage: Primary brand colour — headers, navigation, document title bars, hero backgrounds. Conveys precision and technology-first positioning.

secondary.name: Electric Teal
secondary.usage: Accent highlights, AI detection frames, active-status indicators, CTAs, data visualisation accents

accent.name: Bright Cyan
accent.usage: UI glow effects, AI vision overlays, hover states, secondary highlights

dark.name: Cool Dark Grey
dark.usage: Body text, secondary headings, dark-mode backgrounds

light.name: Cool Light Grey
light.usage: Section backgrounds, card surfaces — clean clinical backdrop

white.name: White
white.usage: Primary background — emphasises precision of sensing technology

heading.usage: H1, H2, H3 headlines, wordmark — geometric sans-serif chosen for clarity in digital and print

body.usage: Body text, technical documentation, website copy — high legibility for safety specifications

mono.usage: Product codes, model numbers, technical specifications

brand_voice.tone: Plain-spoken expert — talks to operators, not boardrooms
brand_voice.style: Outcome-focused: lives saved, accidents prevented, compliance achieved

key_messages: Eliminate vehicle blind spots and prevent collisions
Patented bbs-tek white-sound — locatable, less noise pollution
AI vision detection that distinguishes humans from objects
Compliant with FORS, CLOCS, DVS Progressive Safe System
Trusted globally by transport, construction, and emergency services
value_propositions: Provide indisputable video evidence for incidents
Engineered for harsh environments — built to last

full.usage: Primary logo for documents, websites, vehicle decals

mark.usage: Icon/mark only for product labels, social media, favicons

imagery.style: Real working environments — HGVs, plant, refuse trucks, depots
imagery.photography: Drivers and operators in-situ, equipment installed on real vehicles, on-the-job action shots
imagery.illustrations: Technical diagrams of camera angles, blind-spot zones, sound directionality

visual_motif.name: AI Detection Frame
visual_motif.description: Teal-accented bounding boxes and scan-line overlays referencing Brigade's AI vision detection UI — used as graphical dividers, callout frames, and status indicators throughout marketing materials
visual_motif.usage: Page headers, section breaks, CTA buttons, slide accents, data callout frames
visual_motif.style: Thin teal borders with subtle glow or gradient from Electric Teal to Bright Cyan

linkedin.background: solid #0A1E3D (deep navy) with #00C9DB teal accent bar or AI Detection Frame overlay

instagram.background: deep navy with teal AI Detection Frame accents and white text
instagram.style: Modern, technology-first — teal glow accents over deep navy, clean clinical feel

powerpoint.accent_bar: #00C9DB (left edge, full height — AI Detection Frame motif)

do: Use Deep Navy (#0A1E3D) as the primary brand colour, accented with Electric Teal (#00C9DB) and Bright Cyan (#00E5FF)
Apply the AI Detection Frame motif — teal-bordered callout frames and scan-line overlays referencing Brigade's AI vision UI
Use Montserrat Bold for headlines and the wordmark; Open Sans or Inter for body copy
Lead with concrete safety outcomes (lives saved, accidents prevented)
Emphasise the technology-first / AI positioning alongside safety heritage
Cite compliance standards by name (FORS, CLOCS, DVS, BS EN 7010)
Show real vehicles and operators, not stock office photography
Use plain, direct language — no jargon, no marketing fluff
Reference actual product codes (Backeye 360, bbs-tek, Backsense, AI HFR Box) where appropriate
dont: Don't reintroduce safety red or safety yellow — the current brand is deep navy/teal/cyan
Don't use warm colour palettes — the brand is cool, clinical, and tech-forward
Don't use boardroom/financial imagery — Brigade speaks to operations and technology
Don't make claims without evidence — link to test data or case studies
Don't undersell the safety stakes — this product prevents fatalities
Don't use Finabeo-style governance/transformation language — it's not the audience
Don't substitute fonts other than Montserrat / Open Sans / Inter without approval

metadata.notes: Updated 2026-04-16 to reflect Brigade Electronics' current technology-first rebrand: Deep Navy (#0A1E3D) primary, Electric Teal (#00C9DB) and Bright Cyan (#00E5FF) accents — signalling AI and advanced software rather than hardware. Replaces earlier red palette (v0.2) and yellow palette (v0.1). Visual motif shifted from Safety Stripe to AI Detection Frame (teal bounding-box overlays). Typography remains Montserrat / Open Sans.
next_steps: Confirm exact hex values for navy/teal with Brigade brand team
Provide official logo SVGs in updated palette variants
Confirm font licensing for Montserrat in commercial materials
Validate AI HFR Box product name and compliance terminology with product team
